name: Philadelphia Sticky Buns
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------
BOTTOM ICING
1. Combine pecans, butter, and brown sugar for the glaze.
2. Roll out dough into a rectangle about 1/4 to 1/8 inch thick, depending on how many and how large you want to make them.
3. Spread 1 tablespoon of softened butter around, leaving a 1/4-inch edge. Sprinkle cinnamon all over as desired.
4. Sprinkle the brown sugar mixture evenly across the dough. You can also add an equal amount of chopped nuts and raisins.
5. Roll up into a log and cut off about 1-1/2 to 2 inches, placing it in a lightly greased pan. Let rise for 45 minutes.
6. Bake at 350°F for 15 to 20 minutes. When slightly cooled, drizzle with powdered sugar, butter, vanilla extract, and milk mixture.
NOTE: This is the technique I used to cut the rolls into 12 equal pieces. Use a nine-inch shallow quiche pan; grease it well, including the sides. Place pecans in the bottom of the pan, sprinkle brown sugar over them, then place the rolls on top. Bake at 375°F for about 20 minutes—watch carefully as ovens vary. Allow to cool exactly 5 minutes before turning out.
ENJOY!
